1.11a: Mortgage Administration Department, PO Box 89, Queen Street, Cardiff, CF10 1UA
1.11b: Mortgage Administration Department, PO Box 89, Queen Street, Cardiff, CF10 1UA
1.7: Mortgage New Business, PO Box 89, Queen Street, Cardiff, CF10 1UA
Lending where borrower and mortgagor are different requires specific lender instruction.
1.14: Firm may act if person dealing with transaction or family is seller provided: firm is a partnership, no conflict of interest exists, and the conveyancer acting is not the seller or immediate family.
Written confirmation must be sent to and acknowledged by the lender.
Conveyancers must follow Law Society Guidelines and SRA Outcomes-Focused Regulation.
1.15: Firm may act if person dealing with transaction or family is borrower provided: firm is a partnership, no conflict of interest exists, and the conveyancer acting is not the borrower or immediate family.
Written confirmation must be submitted and lender acknowledgement received before proceeding.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's solicitor required only if the firm is not in a legal directory or not registered with the SRA or CLC at the provided address.
3.1.4: Notify Mortgage New Business (section 1.7) if the seller is unrepresented or using in-house legal advisors.
3.1.5: Acceptable ID (List A - Name or Address): Current UK passport, UK Driving License, HMRC Tax Notification (<12 months), EU ID card/passport, Non-EU passport with Visa, DWP benefit notification (<12 months).

Acceptable ID (List B - Address only): Utility bill (Gas/Elec <3 months, Water <12 months), Council Tax bill (<12 months), Government department letter (<12 months), Solicitor confirmation of house purchase with previous proof (<6 months), Mortgage Statement (<12 months), Tenancy agreement (<12 months), Care home letter (<12 months), Bank/Credit Card statement (<3 months, no online prints).
Items marked for List A can verify either name or address, but not both simultaneously.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor required only if the firm is not in a legal directory or not registered with SRA, CLC, or other regulatory body at the provided address.
3.2.4: Notify Mortgage New Business (section 1.7) if the seller is unrepresented or using in-house legal advisors.
4.1: Lender shares valuation report if physical inspection is conducted; remote (desktop) valuations are not shared.
Valuation is for lender use only and should not be relied upon by the borrower.
Conveyancers must compare valuation against mortgage offer and report issues to 1.7.
4.3: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if valuer assumptions are incorrect.
4.5a: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if re-inspection is required.
4.5b: Send Certificate of Title to: Completions Team, Mortgage New Business, PO Box 89, Queen Street, Cardiff, CF10 1UA.
Monies only released on signed and fully completed certificates.
5.1.1: If seller owned property <6 months, provide: seller's seller name/address, amount paid by seller, details of connections between all parties, work carried out between transactions, transaction dates, and reason for quick sale.
Do not proceed until written lender confirmation is received.
5.2.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if the seller is not the owner or registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports required if adverse search entry exists or if recommended by valuer.
5.4.5: Personal searches accepted if conveyancer provides unqualified certificate of title at own risk and search firm is PCCB Search Code registered.
5.4.6: Search insurance: Not accepted for purchases.
Search insurance: Accepted for remortgages if title is good and marketable and section 9 requirements are met.
5.5.3a: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) regarding breach of planning or building conditions.
5.5.3b: Do not send planning permissions; pass to borrower.
5.5.3c: Do not send building regulation consents; pass to borrower.
5.5.3d: Do not send certificates of lawful use; pass to borrower.
5.5.4: Advise Mortgage New Business (1.7) of restrictions affecting value or marketability and await response before proceeding.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if no more than 25% of the total floor area.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted. Contact 1.7 for further discussion.
5.7.1c: Report freehold flats or flying freeholds to Mortgage New Business (1.7).
5.8.1: Building converted to max 4 flats (one borrower-occupied) accepted if lease has maintenance covenants; report borrower interests in other flats in the building.
5.8.5: Cross-over leaseholds accepted if lease requires simultaneous freehold transfer on sale or unexpired term is >=85 years plus mortgage term.
Term <85 years + mortgage requires extension or variation for simultaneous transfer.
Blocks with >2 flats require a management company.
Lender requires a charge over the borrower's leasehold interest and their freehold interest in the other flat.
5.10.1: Report use restrictions to Mortgage New Business (1.7) and await decision.
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from family (spouse, parent, sibling, child, grandparent, grandchild, legal guardian) are accepted if not repayable.
Gifts from unrelated third parties, friends, or employers are not accepted.
Giftor letter required stating name/address, relationship, borrower details, property address, amount, source, and confirming no interest in property and non-repayability.
Bankruptcy searches required for all giftors; report non-clear searches to 1.7.
Report if family member giftor is also the vendor.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term is 85 years. Notify lender if term is 85 years or less.
5.14.9: Ground rent and fees must be reasonable; RPI-linked increases are generally acceptable; unreasonable multipliers are not accepted.
Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if in doubt.
5.14.10: Service charge matters to be referred to Mortgage New Business (1.7).
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms accepted if unqualified CoT is possible and section 9 met; record policy details on CoT.
5.14.12: Do not send ground rent/service charge receipts; pass to borrower.
5.14.13: Do not send notice of service evidence; pass to borrower.
5.14.15a: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) regarding absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords accepted if unqualified CoT is possible; send copy of policy to lender.
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges must exclude Law of Property Act s121, or include a 28-day mortgagee protection clause, or owner must be a resident-controlled management company.
If rent charge requirements not met, indemnity insurance is required at customer's expense; decline if insurance unavailable.
Rent charges pre-1977 require proof of payment or indemnity insurance.
5.15.2b: Do not send management company share certificates; pass to borrower.
5.15.2c: Do not send signed blank stock transfer forms; pass to borrower.
5.15.2d: Do not send management company Mem & Arts; pass to borrower.
5.16.2: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if unable to certify search entries.
5.17.5a: Send certified copy of Power of Attorney with Certificate of Title.
5.17.5b: Send copy of statutory declaration of non-revocation with TID after charge registration.
5.19.1: Shared Ownership: Wales only; Pobl Group or Shared Ownership Wales template leases only.
Shared Ownership requirements: Lessor's written consent, Mortgagee Protection Clause, >=85 years term, in borrower names only.
Downward staircasing requires written pre-approval; proceeds paid to lender.
Nomination/pre-emption rights accepted if period <=20 weeks.
Section 106 agreements: Summary of points/risks/advice required; do not proceed until instructions received.
All shared housing queries to be referred to Mortgage New Business (1.7).
5.20.1: Report if solar PV panel lease does not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: No lending if solar PV lease fails UK Finance minimum requirements.
Post-completion requests for solar PV to be sent to Mortgage Administration (1.11a).
5.20.4: Disclose existing Green Deal Plans to Mortgage New Business (1.7).
6.1.3: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if borrower does not take up offer.
6.2.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) regarding property description discrepancies.
6.3.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) regarding purchase price issues.
6.4.4: CML Disclosure of Incentives Form must be checked.
Advise lender prior to exchange if deposit is not from borrower's own funds.
Specify nature and amount of all incentives/cashbacks/allowances; lender may reconsider lending if price does not reflect incentives.
6.4.5: Whole mortgage advance must be paid directly to seller's conveyancer unless offer states otherwise.
Notify 1.7 if conveyancer will not have control over all purchase money.
6.5.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if vacant possession is not provided.
6.6.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) regarding properties to be let.
6.6.2: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if letting details are missing.
6.6.3: Do not send tenancy agreements; pass to borrower.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not permitted.
6.7.1: Acceptable Warranty Schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal (to 30/09/09), HAPM, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans, LABC, Checkmate/Castle 10, Build-Zone (must include outbuildings), ICW, Advantage HCI, Protek, AEDIS (Homeproof), Q Policy, ABC+, Global Warranties Ltd, Ark, One Guarantee, CADIS.
Conveyancer must confirm level of cover meets rebuilding cost.
6.7.2: Do not send new home warranty docs; pass to borrower and keep copy on file.
6.7.3: Do not send building standards assignments; pass to borrower and keep copy on file.
6.7.4: If no warranty: confirm monitoring by professional consultant, consultant's experience/qualifications, appropriate indemnity level (6.7.5), and completion of Professional Consultant's Certificate.
Obtain written lender confirmation before submitting Certificate of Title.
6.7.6: Do not send Professional Consultant's Certificate; pass to borrower and keep copy on file.
6.8.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if no agreement/bond for unadopted roads or sewers.
6.9.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if easements are absent.
Indemnity insurance required if defect cannot be rectified; policy details must be on CoT.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments released to the conveyancer.
6.11.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) regarding redevelopment or road proposals.
6.12.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) regarding pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options.
6.13.1: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) regarding undischarged improvement/repair grants.
7.3: Consent required from all occupants aged 17 or over using the pro-forma incorporated in the Mortgage Deed.
7.4: Contact Mortgage New Business (1.7) if doubts exist regarding information accuracy.
8.1: Conveyancer may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity policy must be sent to lender or insurer and policy number noted on the CoT.
9.2: Indemnity limit: Full market value preferred; minimum of loan advance amount accepted.
10.2b: Advance sent to client account on the working day prior to completion.
10.3: Standard deductions detailed in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: Return advance via CHAPS if completion delayed beyond 48 hours.
If delayed beyond offer expiry, new offer/documentation may be required.
10.9: Contact Completions Team, Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) if completion is delayed.
10.10: Maximum hold period for mortgage advance: 2 days.
10.11: Interest charged to borrower from the date the advance is released at the account rate.
12.3.1: Contact Completions Team, Mortgage New Business (0330 333 4021) for release of retentions.
14.1.4: Form CH2 required for instalment mortgages or where a retention is made.
14.1.5: Send original Mortgage Deed and TID to the lender.
14.2.1: Send title deeds to: Mortgage Administration Department, PO Box 89, Queen Street, Cardiff, CF10 1UA.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents to send: TID, original Mortgage Deed, Occupier Consent Forms, and required indemnity policies.
Other documents should be sent to the borrowers.
16.1.1: Contact Mortgage Administration Department (1.11a) for title documents.
16.3.1: No standard form of transfer/deed of covenant provided.
16.3.2: Contact Mortgage Administration (1.11a) for debt amount.
16.3.4: Do not send Transfer of Equity; file with HM Land Registry.
16.3.7a: Contact Mortgage Administration (1.11a) for execution of transfer of equity.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: Executed as a deed by common seal of Principality Building Society by order of Board in presence of Authorised Official.
16.4.1: Contact Mortgage Administration (1.11a) for consent to letting.
16.4.2: Send copy of proposed tenancy agreement to the lender.
16.5.2: Contact Mortgage Administration (1.11a) to confirm deeds/agreements do not affect security.
16.5.3a: Send Deed of Variation to Mortgage Administration (1.11a); may be referred to valuer.
16.5.3b: Send Deed of Rectification to Mortgage Administration (1.11a).
16.5.3c: Send Deed of Easement to Mortgage Administration (1.11a).
16.5.3d: Send Option Agreements to Mortgage Administration (1.11a).
17.1.1: Contact Mortgage Administration (1.11a) for redemption statements.
17.2.1a: Send discharge and repayment remittance to Mortgage Administration (1.11a).
17.2.1b: Lender discharges via e-DS1 directly with Land Registry; DS1 form not required.
